Politics.headline: Trump Tries to Change Narrative at State of the Union Address
Politics.detail: As President Donald Trump prepares to deliver his State of the Union address, public sentiment appears to be turning against him, giving him an opportunity to rewrite the script, particularly concerning economic progress, despite a Supreme Court setback regarding his tariffs. The speech is seen as a chance for Trump to rally his base for the upcoming midterm elections in 2026, particularly after a Supreme Court ruling challenged his tariff policies.

Politics.headline: California Democrats Can't Pick a Favorite as 9 Candidates Vie to Replace Newsom
Politics.detail: Nine Democrats are competing for California's gubernatorial seat, creating a challenge for the party to consolidate support. The candidates recently participated in a convention, with Eric Swalwell leading in preliminary results, though no candidate secured the party's endorsement.

Politics.headline: Josh Shapiro to Neighbors: Get Off My Lawn
Politics.detail: Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro is currently involved in a dispute with his neighbor, which has escalated into a political issue. The details of the dispute and its specific political implications are still emerging.

Politics.headline: Trump Imposes New Tariffs, Denounces Supreme Court Justices as a 'Disgrace' After Ruling
Politics.detail: Following a Supreme Court decision against his tariff regime, President Trump has announced he will impose new tariffs and criticized the justices involved. He has also increased global tariffs to 15% after the Supreme Court ruled a bulk of import taxes were illegal.

Politics.headline: JD or Marco? Trump Asking Advisers About 2028
Politics.detail: Former President Trump is reportedly consulting with advisors about potential candidates for the 2028 election, including JD Vance and Marco Rubio. This comes as Trump faces scrutiny over his recent tariff decisions and relationships with other countries.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump Warns Netflix of 'Consequences' Unless it Pulls Top Democrat from Board
Finance & Economy.detail: Former President Trump is putting pressure on Netflix, demanding they remove a prominent Democrat from their board or face repercussions.

Finance & Economy.headline: China Overtakes US as Germany's Top Trading Partner
Finance & Economy.detail: Germany's trade relationship sees a shift as China surpasses the United States to become its largest trading partner.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Stocks Swing as Trump Hits Back with 10% Tariff
Finance & Economy.detail: Following a court ruling against his previous tariffs, former President Trump announced a new 10% global tariff, causing market fluctuations.

Finance & Economy.headline: Americans see bigger tax refunds so far this year as filing season begins at a slower pace
Finance & Economy.detail: The IRS indicates that Americans are receiving larger tax refunds early in the filing season, but the overall pace of filings has been slower compared to previous years.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks slide after Trump threatens new tariffs over Greenland
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. and European markets experienced a downturn, and Wall Street's "fear gauge" spiked after Trump suggested new tariffs related to Greenland.

Technology.headline: The splinternet: how online shutdowns are getting cheaper and easier to impose.
Technology.detail: Analysis of how online shutdowns are becoming more prevalent and affordable to implement, leading to increased fragmentation of the internet.

Technology.headline: Fairphone 6 review: Cheaper, repairable and longer-lasting Android
Technology.detail: A review of the Fairphone 6, highlighting its affordability, focus on repairability, and commitment to long-term software support.

Technology.headline: Google Pixel 10 Pro Fold review: Dust-resistant and more durable foldable phone
Technology.detail: A review of the Google Pixel 10 Pro Fold, praising its dust resistance and improved durability compared to previous foldable models.

Technology.headline: Nothing Phone (4a) series coming to India on March 5: Price, specs and other details
Technology.detail: Details on the upcoming launch of the Nothing Phone (4a) series in India, including the expected price, specifications, and other relevant information.

Technology.headline: OpenAI developing AI devices including smart speaker: Report
Technology.detail: Report that OpenAI is expanding beyond software and developing AI-powered devices, including a smart speaker.

Science.headline: Pecan Consumption Improves Cardiovascular Health and Diet Quality, New Review Shows
Science.detail: A new scientific review analyzing over 20 years of research suggests that pecans may have significant heart health benefits and improve overall diet quality. Scientists found consistent evidence that eating pecans can improve cardiovascular health.

Science.headline: A simple water shift could turn Arctic farmland into a carbon sink
Science.detail: New research from northern Norway indicates that raising the water level in cultivated Arctic peatlands can significantly reduce carbon dioxide emissions, and in some cases, even enable the land to absorb more CO₂ than it releases. Restoring higher groundwater levels could flip northern farmland from a carbon source into a climate ally.

Science.headline: NASA delays Artemis II moon mission to April over rocket system issue
Science.detail: NASA has delayed the Artemis II mission to the moon to at least April due to a new issue with the rocket's helium system. The mission, originally scheduled for March 6, would have been the first crewed lunar flight in over 50 years.

Science.headline: Quantum reservoir computing peaks at the edge of many-body chaos, study suggests
Science.detail: A new study suggests that quantum reservoir computing, a type of machine learning, performs optimally at the boundary of many-body chaos. This finding could have implications for the development of more efficient quantum computers.

Science.headline: Triceratops' massive head may have been doing more than just showing off those famous horns
Science.detail: Using CT scans and 3D reconstructions of fossil skulls, researchers uncovered a surprisingly complex nasal system hidden inside its enormous snout. Triceratops' massive head may have been doing more than just showing off those famous horns.

Health.headline: My daughter turns 18 today. I'm giving her the gift of shared caring responsibilities with her brothers.
Health.detail: A parent reflects on instilling values of care and responsibility in their children as their daughter reaches adulthood.

Health.headline: A long-acting HIV drug arrives in Zimbabwe for some at highest risk
Health.detail: Zimbabwe is one of the first countries to roll out lenacapavir, a long-acting HIV prevention drug that only needs to be administered twice a year, offering hope in slowing new infections, especially among high-risk groups.

Health.headline: Ibuprofen and paracetamol: how everyday painkillers ended up at the heart of a looming global health crisis
Health.detail: Experts are warning that the interplay between common painkillers and antimicrobial resistance could have far-reaching consequences, potentially undermining the effectiveness of crucial antibiotics and jeopardizing the treatment of everything from routine infections to life-threatening diseases.

Health.headline: Scientific journal backtracks on apple cider vinegar weight-loss study.
Health.detail: A scientific journal has retracted a study about apple cider vinegar and weight loss.

Health.headline: 'We want to rebuild trust': fired CDC workers form group to combat Trump's war on science.
Health.detail: Fired CDC workers form a group to combat Trump's war on science.

Sports.headline: Wembanyama, Spurs win 8 straight, Kings lose 16 in a row
Sports.detail: The San Antonio Spurs, led by Wembanyama, secured their eighth consecutive victory, while the Sacramento Kings suffered their 16th straight loss.

Sports.headline: Ryan Garcia dominates Mario Barrios to win WBC welterweight title
Sports.detail: Ryan Garcia put on a dominant performance against Mario Barrios to claim the WBC welterweight title.

Sports.headline: Jasprit Bumrah's magical slower ball: The last thing Ryan Rickleton saw was a doorknob turning
Sports.detail: An analysis of Jasprit Bumrah's deceptive slower ball in a recent T20 World Cup match.

Sports.headline: Eileen Gu defends Olympic ski halfpipe gold, makes it 6 medals in 6 events over Winter Games career
Sports.detail: Eileen Gu won the gold medal in the ski halfpipe at the Winter Olympics, bringing her total medal count to six across six events.

Sports.headline: Premier League: Manchester City keeps pressure on leader Arsenal with win over Newcastle
Sports.detail: Manchester City secured a victory over Newcastle, maintaining pressure on Arsenal, who currently lead the Premier League.

Entertainment.headline: Daniel Radcliffe: New Harry Potter Actor 'Will Be Better Than Me'
Entertainment.detail: Daniel Radcliffe expressed his belief that the actor cast as Harry Potter in the upcoming HBO television adaptation will surpass his own performance, as a new generation prepares to enter the Wizarding World.

Entertainment.headline: American salsa legend Willie Colón dies at age 75
Entertainment.detail: Pioneering trombonist, vocalist, and composer Willie Colón passed away on Saturday at the age of 75, according to a statement from his family.

Entertainment.headline: Stars arrive for the UK's BAFTA film awards
Entertainment.detail: Stars are gathering for the British Academy Film Awards, a key event in the awards season leading up to the Oscars.

Entertainment.headline: Snooki's Health Journey: Updates After Nicole Polizzi's Cervical Cancer Diagnosis
Entertainment.detail: The 'Jersey Shore' star revealed her diagnosis with adenocarcinoma in a February 2026 TikTok video and is sharing updates on her health journey.

Entertainment.headline: Rishab Shetty's Jai Hanuman goes on floors; Hanu-Man star Teja Sajja attends film's puja ceremony
Entertainment.detail: Rishab Shetty has begun shooting for 'Jai Hanuman,' with Teja Sajja, the star of 'Hanu-Man,' attending the film's puja ceremony.

Crime & Justice.headline: Northamptonshire Police name victim of stabbing murder in town skate park
Crime & Justice.detail: Mason Miler, 20, was killed in an attack on Wednesday at a skate park in Ringway in the Briar Hill area of Northampton; a 17-year-old was also attacked and remains in hospital with life-threatening injuries, and seven people have been arrested in connection with the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: California launches civil rights probe into botched evacuations in historically Black Altadena
Crime & Justice.detail: California Atty. Gen. Rob Bonta announced an investigation into potential civil rights violations during the emergency response to the Eaton fire where West Altadena, a historically Black community, received late evacuation alerts and few firefighting resources.

Crime & Justice.headline: New Mexico AG reopens Epstein ranch probe
Crime & Justice.detail: New Mexico’s attorney general has reopened an investigation into activities at a ranch once owned by convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

Crime & Justice.headline: FBI Boston Warns of Nihilistic Violent Extremists Targeting Children and Vulnerable Victims Online
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI has issued a warning about Nihilistic Violent Extremists targeting children and vulnerable victims online.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former NFL Player and Laboratory Owner Convicted in $328M Genetic Testing Fraud Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: A former NFL player and laboratory owner has been convicted in a $328 million genetic testing fraud scheme.

Education.headline: IIT Madras Rolls Out BSc In Management And Data Science For Students And Working Professionals
Education.detail: IIT Madras has launched a new BSc program in Management and Data Science for students and working professionals, aiming to provide accessible higher education opportunities in these fields.

Education.headline: ISRO Launches YUVIKA 2026 To Inspire School Students In Space Science
Education.detail: ISRO has launched the YUVIKA 2026 program to inspire school students in the field of space science, encouraging them to pursue careers in STEM.

Education.headline: Delhi Launches Online Application For Private School Seats Under RTE Quota
Education.detail: The Delhi government has initiated the online application process for private school seats available under the Right to Education (RTE) quota, ensuring access to education for economically weaker sections.

Education.headline: Dhaka University VC Niaz Ahmed Khan resigns
Education.detail: Dhaka University Vice-Chancellor Professor Niaz Ahmed Khan has resigned and handed in his resignation to Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon. No official reason has been disclosed for the resignation.

Education.headline: Why adults pursuing career growth or personal interests are the 'new majority' student
Education.detail: Millions of U.S. adults enroll in credit and non-credit college courses to earn professional certificates, learn new skills or to pursue academic degrees because they are seeking career advancement, higher pay and job security, while others want to explore their personal interests or try new things.

Lifestyle.headline: Nobody talks about why retired women clean the house before anyone visits but sit in silence the rest of the week—it's because being needed and being seen became the same thing forty years ago and they never learned to separate the two
Lifestyle.detail: This article discusses how retired women often feel the need to clean and perform for others, stemming from a societal expectation that their worth is tied to being needed and seen. It explores how to break free from this pattern and find worth in simply existing.

Lifestyle.headline: Divorce doesn't have to be ugly
Lifestyle.detail: This article provides advice on how to handle divorce in a way that minimizes the negative impact on children, emphasizing the importance of prioritizing their needs and avoiding conflict with the ex-spouse in front of them. It suggests maintaining routines, allowing children to express their feelings, and seeking counseling if needed.

Lifestyle.headline: Lifestyle Features from Sunday, February 22nd, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Your Harrogate provides local news, including a pilot's death after a crash near Masham, a charity football tournament hosted by Ripon's Portly Pig, and a review of parking charges across North Yorkshire by the council. They also offer half-price vouchers for local attractions and dining.

Lifestyle.headline: Horoscope today: Your daily guide for Sunday, February 22, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Claire Petulengro, an astrologer, provides horoscopes for each zodiac sign, offering guidance on relationships, career, and personal well-being. The horoscopes encourage readers to prioritize self-care, address fears, and maintain professionalism.

Lifestyle.headline: Modern parenting means apps for sports, school and more. What this means for data privacy.
Lifestyle.detail: A recent article discusses the increasing reliance on apps in modern parenting for managing sports, school, and other activities, while also raising concerns about data privacy implications. It highlights the need for parents to be aware of how their children's data is being collected and used by these apps.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Legal prostitution? A new legislative low
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Colorado Democrats have introduced a bill to decriminalize “commercial sexual activity” with no limits on prostitution, solicitation, businesses, patronizing or public displays, raising concerns about exploitation and sex trafficking.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Stand back, Congress needs a second Supreme Court jolt
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Supreme Court's ruling on tariffs blocked Trump but insufficiently addressed executive branch overreach, highlighting the need for Congress to reclaim its constitutional powers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Let's see the receipts on data centers
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Pennsylvania's tax exemption for data centers lacks transparency and measurable results, making it difficult to assess its full impact and justify its cost to taxpayers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial cartoons for Sunday, Feb. 22
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A collection of editorial cartoons providing a satirical take on current events.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Pa. doesn't need to bend over backward for data center developers
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Pennsylvania should enforce strict standards to protect communities and the environment, as the data center boom may become a bust, and the state doesn't need to give up potentially $2 billion in tax revenue to be competitive.

World.headline: Armed man shot and killed inside Trump's Florida resort Mar-a-Lago: Secret Service
World.detail: A man in his 20s was shot and killed by Secret Service agents after unlawfully entering a secure perimeter at President Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort in Palm Beach, Florida; Trump was not present at the time.

World.headline: Trump Hikes New US Global Tariff Rate to 15%
World.detail: President Trump has increased the global tariff rate to 15% after the Supreme Court struck down his previous tariffs; he also launched a personal attack on the conservative justices who ruled against him.

World.headline: Pakistan launches deadly strikes in Afghanistan
World.detail: Pakistan conducted strikes along the border with Afghanistan targeting hideouts of Pakistani militants, which they blame for recent attacks inside the country. The overnight attacks were the most extensive since border clashes in October.

World.headline: Iran designates naval, air forces of EU states 'terrorist entities' in tit-for-tat move
World.detail: Iran has designated the armed forces of European Union member states as 'terrorist entities' in a reciprocal move, following the EU's decision to designate the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a 'terrorist organization'.

World.headline: Chinese New Year celebration events held across world
World.detail: Celebrations for the Chinese New Year have taken place in various locations around the world, including parades and traditional performances.

Zimbabwe.headline: Ziyambi claim of a diaspora vote under the new constitutional amendments is a legal deception
Zimbabwe.detail: Minister of Justice Ziyambi Ziyambi is accused of presenting a radical dismantling of the electoral process as a 'historically transformative' reform by dangling the diaspora vote, which has been denied to millions for decades, but this claim is a calculated insult to the intelligence of the nation. The claim that stripping the Zimbabwean citizenry of their right to directly elect a President is the key to unlocking the diaspora vote is a deception, as the barrier to the diaspora vote has always been the residency of the voter, not the office being contested.

Zimbabwe.headline: Epworth welcomes new HIV prevention drug
Zimbabwe.detail: Young women, mothers holding babies and some men lined up in a dusty field on the outskirts of Zimbabwe's capital, Harare, for injections of lenacapavir, a new HIV prevention drug launched in the country. Zimbabwe is one of the first countries to roll out this long-acting drug that authorities hope will slow new infections, with clinical studies demonstrating near-total protection for high-risk groups.

Zimbabwe.headline: Online Exchange Erupts Between Chris Brown's Former Partner and Rumoured Girlfriend
Zimbabwe.detail: An online exchange has erupted between Chris Brown's former partner and a rumoured girlfriend. The details of the exchange were not specified in the provided context.

Zimbabwe.headline: Banking: High interest rates, tight liquidity choke industry
Zimbabwe.detail: Local companies are struggling to access capital as traditional avenues for long-term funding remain effectively closed or severely limited, leaving the productive sector to suffer. This is due to high interest rates and tight liquidity.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bulawayo baby snatcher revealed as a female Form One pupil
Zimbabwe.detail: A Bulawayo teenager has been arrested after a toddler was abducted from a city mall. The suspect has been identified as a female Form One pupil.
